title: Tral Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Tral Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Tral Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Tral town in the Pulwama district of the Indian Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.
overview.description: Tral Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Tral town in the Pulwama district of the Indian Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is located about 26 km from Pulwama town, the district headquarter and 44 km from Union Territory Capital Srinagar. It was formed on 26 October 2019, for the protection of the endangered hangul in the region. It is spread over an area of 154.15 km2 (59.52 sq mi) and came into being by merging Panyar-Shikargah and Khiram wildlife conservation reserves and few other forest compartments of Awantipora forest division.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Pahalgam
topPlaces.description: Pahalgam (Urdu pronunciation: [pɛɦɛlɡɑːm]) or Pahalgom (Kashmiri pronunciation: [pəhəlʲɡoːm]) is a town in Anantnag district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is located on the banks of Lidder River at an altitude of 2,200 m (7,200 ft) in the Vale of Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Tral
topPlaces.description: Tral is a town, sub-district, and a notified area committee in the Pulwama district of the Indian state of Jammu and Kashmir. The town is situated at a distance of 40 kilometres (25 mi) from the summer capital, Srinagar and 26 kilometres (16 mi) from district headquarters, Pulwama.

topPlaces.name: Gufkral
topPlaces.description: Gufkral ('guf' means cave and 'kral' means potter) is a site inhabited by potters who utilize the caves. Caves and archeological site are located at Banmir village in the Tral Tehsil of the Pulwama District, in the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India.

topPlaces.name: Midoora
topPlaces.description: Midoora( میڈورہ) also known as Midur(میڈُر) , is a piedmont, agglomerated rural settlement in the Awantipora Tehsil of Pulwama district in Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir. It is at a distance of 30 km from Srinagar and 8 km from the main town Tral and 7 km from Awantipora.

items.note: Pahalgam (Urdu pronunciation: [pɛɦɛlɡɑːm]) or Pahalgom (Kashmiri pronunciation: [pəhəlʲɡoːm]) is a town in Anantnag district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is located on the banks of Lidder River at an altitude of 2,200 m (7,200 ft) in the Vale of Kashmir.

Tral is a town, sub-district, and a notified area committee in the Pulwama district of the Indian state of Jammu and Kashmir. The town is situated at a distance of 40 kilometres (25 mi) from the summer capital, Srinagar and 26 kilometres (16 mi) from district headquarters, Pulwama.

Gufkral ('guf' means cave and 'kral' means potter) is a site inhabited by potters who utilize the caves. Caves and archeological site are located at Banmir village in the Tral Tehsil of the Pulwama District, in the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India.

Midoora( میڈورہ) also known as Midur(میڈُر) , is a piedmont, agglomerated rural settlement in the Awantipora Tehsil of Pulwama district in Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir. It is at a distance of 30 km from Srinagar and 8 km from the main town Tral and 7 km from Awantipora.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tral Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tral Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tral Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (31 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (113 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tral Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tral Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tral Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Tral Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tral Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pahalgam, Tral, Gufkral, Midoora.
